description: 22-field record format used by SecretBench (Basak et al., MSR 2023). Mirrored from https://github.com/setu1421/SecretBench. Used by both the mirror corpus generator and the real-dataset scorer so the runner is identical for both inputs.
citation: Basak, S. K., Neil, L., Reaves, B., Williams, L. (2023). SecretBench: A Dataset of Software Secrets. 20th IEEE/ACM International Conference on Mining Software Repositories (MSR), 347-351. doi:10.1109/MSR59073.2023.00053. arXiv:2303.06729.

fields.name: id
fields.note: Stable unique identifier for the secret record.

fields.name: secret
fields.note: The literal secret text the scanner is asked to surface.

fields.name: repo_name
fields.note: owner/repo of the origin GitHub repository.

fields.name: commit_id
fields.note: Git SHA at which the secret was observed.

fields.name: file_path
fields.note: Relative path inside the repository to the file containing the secret.

fields.name: start_line
fields.note: 1-indexed first line of the secret span.

fields.name: end_line
fields.note: 1-indexed last line; equal to start_line for single-line secrets.

fields.name: start_column
fields.note: 0-indexed column where the secret starts on start_line.

fields.name: end_column
fields.note: 0-indexed column where the secret ends on end_line.

fields.name: label
fields.note: true = manually confirmed real secret; false = false positive reported by a scanner but determined to be a non-secret.

fields.name: category
fields.note: SecretBench taxonomy bucket (e.g. 'authentication-key', 'cryptographic-private-key', 'cloud-service-credential', 'generic-high-entropy-string', 'database-connection-string', 'webhook-url-token').

fields.name: comment
fields.note: Free-text reviewer note. Often the rationale for the label, or the placeholder pattern that triggered the false-positive flag.

fields.name: entropy
fields.note: Shannon entropy of the secret string in bits/byte.

fields.name: character_set
fields.note: One of 'alphanumeric', 'base64', 'hex', 'mixed', 'symbolic' — coarse alphabet bucket.

fields.name: has_words
fields.note: true if the secret contains an English-dictionary word substring; usually correlates with placeholders.

fields.name: length
fields.note: Byte length of the raw secret string.

fields.name: is_template
fields.note: true if the surrounding line looks like a template placeholder (e.g. {{ token }} or <YOUR_KEY_HERE>).

fields.name: is_multiline
fields.note: true for PEM blocks, multi-line JSON service-account keys, etc.

fields.name: in_url
fields.note: true if the secret appears as a URL component (path, query, basic-auth).

fields.name: committer_email
fields.note: Email of the commit author (may be redacted to a domain-only form in the mirror corpus).

fields.name: commit_date
fields.note: ISO-8601 commit timestamp.

fields.name: domain
fields.note: If in_url=true, the host portion of the URL.

fields.name: file_type
fields.note: File extension or shebang-derived type (e.g. 'yaml', 'json', 'py', 'go', 'env', 'Dockerfile').
